methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea is a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Christopher Amidon that has raised $10M+. The valuation universe consists of five private peers in the ocean tech sector: Clippership, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics, Albacore, and Odin Dynamics. No direct public comparables exist in this frontier subsector.

A stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy. Applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $25M. This reflects the expected step-up in company value upon progression beyond the current seed stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method given limited public market data and the early stage of the company. Key uncertainties include the comparability of private peer valuations, capital efficiency relative to the peer set, and the company's path to revenue. Given the frontier nature of subsea technology, execution risk and market adoption timing remain material variables not fully captured in stage-based mechanics alone.
